qText: Two friends, Sachin and Kuldeep, rent a stable. Sachin rents 21 horses for 6 months and 15 cows for 2 months. Kuldeep rents 15 cows for 6 months and 40 sheep for 7.5 months. If 3 horses eat as much food as 5 cows, and 6 cows eat as much as 10 sheep, what share of the rent should Sachin pay ?
P, Q, and R invest ‚āĻ20,000, ‚āĻ30,000, and ‚āĻ40,000, respectively. P invests for 8 months, Q for 12 months, and R for 10 months. The total profit is ‚āĻ46,000. What is P's share of the profit?

P and Q start a partnership. P invests ‚āĻ50,000. After 4 months, Q joins with ‚āĻ75,000. After 2 more months, P doubles his capital. If the business runs for 12 months, find the profit-sharing ratio of P to Q .
Mayur and Aham enter into a partnership by making investments in the ratio 2 : 3. 10% of the total profit goes to charity. If Aham's share is ‚āĻ4,860, then what is the total profit (in ‚āĻ) earned ?
Nikita made two investments; she earns 10% on one investment and lost 20% on the second investment. If the investments were in the ratio 2 : 3, then she got a :
A lady invests ‚āĻ27,600 in three shares in the ratio 3 : 4 : 5 which pay dividends of 20%, 15% and 5% on her investment for that year, respectively. What is the total dividend ?
Swati starts a business with ‚āĻ 4,200 and after 5 months, Bani joins Swati as her partner. After a year, the profit is divided among Swati and Bani in the ratio 2 : 5. What is Bani's contribution ?

Three persons A, B and C invested some money in a business. The share of A is twice the share of B, and the share of B is thrice the share of C. After 1 year, they earned ‚āĻ6,000. Find the money A received.
Three partners P, Q and R invest ‚āĻ 1,000, 1,200 and 1,500, respectively, in ‚āĻ ‚āĻ a business. What should be the share of Q if the total profit is 666? ‚āĻ

Kapil initiated a business with an investment of ‚āĻ30,00,000 in April 2022. After six months, his friend Kunal joined him with an investment of ‚āĻ25,00,000. In February 2023, Kapil's Ex-Class fellow Saloni also invested an amount equal to the total investment made by the earlier two partners. In what ratio the profit earned after 3 years from the date on which Kapil initiated the business, will be distributed ?

A is a working partner and B is a sleeping partner in a business. A invested ‚āĻ30,000 and B invested ‚āĻ50,000. A receives 20% of the profit for managing the business and the rest is divided in proportion to their capital. What does B get out of the profit of ‚āĻ10,000?

X, Y and Z enter into a partnership. X invests some money at the beginning. Y invests thrice the amount of X after 4 months and Z invest double the amount of Y after 9 months from the beginning. If the annual gain be Rs. 450000, then what is the share of Y ?

Sonu started a business by investing ‚āĻ40,000. After 4 months, Prem joined with ‚āĻ80,000. At the end of 8 months from the start, Alok joined with ‚āĻ1,20,000. If the total profit is ‚āĻ1,56,000 at the end of the year, find the share of Alok .
P and Q start a business. P invests ‚āĻ40,000 for 9 months, Q invests ‚āĻ60,000 for 6 months. What is Q's share of a ‚āĻ45,000 profit ?
A and B invest ‚āĻ20,000 and ‚āĻ35,000 respectively in a business. What is A's share in a profit of ‚āĻ25,000?
P and Q contribute to a business investment in the ratio 3:5. They earn a profit of ‚āĻ66,000. P is a working partner and receives 10% of the profit extra. What is P's total share ?
A and B invested ‚āĻ1,40,000 and ‚āĻ1,80,000 respectively. A remained for 10 months and B for 7 months. What is the total profit if B's share is ‚āĻ15,201 ?

Two individuals, A and B, rent a pasture. A uses 18 cows for a period of 3 months and 20 sheep for 4 months, and B uses 35 sheep for 6 months. If 3 cows are equal to 6 sheep, determine A's share of the rent.
Pankaj, Kuldeep, and Ankit invested ‚āĻ25,000, ‚āĻ35,000, and ‚āĻ40,000 respectively in a business. If the profit at the end of the year was ‚āĻ30,000, find Kuldeep's share.

X and Y started a business by investing ‚āĻ48,000 and ‚āĻ72,000 respectively. After 6 months, X withdrew half of his capital, while Y increased his capital by ‚āĻ24,000. At the end of the year, they earned a total profit of ‚āĻ55,000. What is Y's share in the profit?

Komal and Koyal started a business with ‚āĻ1,20,000 and ‚āĻ80,000 respectively. After 6 months, Koyal added ‚āĻ40,000 more. Komal withdrew ‚āĻ40,000 at the same time. What is the profit-sharing ratio at the end of 1 year?

Ankit invested ‚āĻ20,000 and Shakti invested ‚āĻ30,000. The total profit was ‚āĻ80,000. Ankit receives 25% of the total profit as a management fee. The remaining profit is divided in proportion to the investments. What is Ankit's total share?
A, B, and C invested ‚āĻ30,000, ‚āĻ50,000, and ‚āĻ90,000 respectively in a business. If the total profit at the end of the year is ‚āĻ30,000, what is A's share of the profit?

X and Y share a rented field. X utilizes 15 horses for 5 months, while Y uses 25 cows for 4 months and 40 sheep for 5 months. If 2 horses are equivalent to 5 cows, and 3 cows are equal to 9 sheep. What portion of the rent is X pay?

A and B start a business. A invests 2 times more than B. After 6 months, A withdraws half of his capital, and B triples his capital. If the total profit after one year is ‚āĻ65,000, find the share of A.

Ravi, Aman, and Karan started a business together. They invested ‚āĻ40,000, ‚āĻ60,000, and ‚āĻ80,000 respectively for one year. If the total profit at the end of the year is ‚āĻ55,000, what will be Aman's share in the profit ?

A and B invest ‚āĻ50,000 and ‚āĻ1,00,000 respectively, in a business. After one year, the profit is distributed, including simple interest at 10% per annum on the capital. Total profit, including interest, is ‚āĻ30,000. What is A's share ?
Sohan started a business with ‚āĻ80,000. After 5 months, Rohan joined with ‚āĻ1,60,000. After another 4 months, Sohan withdrew ‚āĻ40,000. What is the ratio of their profits at the end of the year?

Two individuals, A and B, rent a pasture together. A has 9 horses that they graze for 2 months. B grazes 12 cows for 3 months and 18 sheep for 2 months. The following relationships are given: ‚óŹ 3 horses are equivalent to 6 cows, ‚óŹ 2 cows are equivalent to 4 sheep. What amount of rent should A pay ?

X, Y, and Z started a business with capitals in the ratio 3:5:8 After 4 months, Z withdrew 50% of his capital. After another 4 months, Y withdrew half of his capital. The business continued for a total of 18 months. If total profit is ‚āĻ9,44,955, find the ratio of profit shares of X : Y : Z.
Rohit invests ‚āĻ50,000 for 12 months, Ramesh invests ‚āĻ1,00,000 for 6 months. What is Rohit's share of ‚āĻ20,000 profit ?

A and B start a business with capitals in the ratio 5 : 3. After 6 months, A withdraws ‚āĻ10,000 and B doubles his investment. If the initial investment of A was ‚āĻ50,000, and the total profit after 1 year is ‚āĻ90,000, find the profit share of A.
Ashish started a business with a sum of ‚āĻ 60,000. Sachin joined him 8 months later with a sum of ‚āĻ 35,000. At what respective ratio will the two share the profit after two years?

Amit invested ‚āĻ90,000 in a business. After 3 months, Sumit joined the partnership with ‚āĻ60,000. After another 3 months, Amit withdrew ‚āĻ30,000. Find the ratio of their profits at the end of the year.
Ram invests ‚āĻ60,000 while Shyam invests ‚āĻ90,000, with Ram's investment lasting for 10 months and Shyam's for 8 months. The total profit amounts to ‚āĻ40,000. What is the Ram's share ?
P and Q invested in a business in the ratio 4 : 5. The profit ratio was 8 : 9. If Q invested his money for 9 months, for how many months did P invest?

P, Q, and R invested 12,000, ‚āĻ16,000, and ‚āĻ20,000 respectively. P's investment was for 12 months, Q's for 8 months, and R's for 4 months. The total profit is ‚āĻ55,000. What is Q's share of the profit ?

Sumit starts a business with ‚āĻ80,000. Three months later, Sachin joined with ‚āĻ100,000. Six months after starting, Sumit increased his investment by ‚āĻ40,000. Sachin leaves the business three months before the end of the year. What is their profit-sharing ratio ?
A and B invest ‚āĻ60,000 and ‚āĻ80,000 respectively. A remains in business for 8 months, B for 9 months. If the total profit is ‚āĻ40,000, what is A's share ?

In a three-way joint venture, the first investor contributes capital, the second contributes time and strategy, and the third brings a network of suppliers. They agree to share profits in the ratio 5 : 6 : 3. If the venture earns Rs. 2,80,000 in the first year, how much should the strategist (second partner) receive ?

The investments of three partners, A, B, and C, are initially in the ratio 5 : 6 : 7. After a year, A adds ‚āĻ40,000, B adds ‚āĻ35,000, and C adds some amount to their respective investments. The new ratio of their investments becomes 7 : 8 : 9. Find the amount (in ‚āĻ) added by partner C.
P, Q, and R invested in a business in the ratio 4 : 3 : 5. After a year, the total profit is Rs.6,00,000. What is Q's share?

A yojana has three stakeholders. P brought 60% of the capital, and Q brought the rest. R joined later and was promised 30% of the total profit for branding. If total profit was Rs. 3,50,000, how much was Q's share ?
If the ratio of capital of A and B is 4 : 2 and the time for which they invested is in the ratio 2 : 4, then the profit ratio is:

Partners A and B entered into a business investing Rs. 40,000 and Rs. 9,0,000 respectively. After 6 months, C joins with Rs. 1,40,000. At year-end, profit was Rs. 80,000. How much more did C earn than A ?
A and B invested Rs. 80,000 and Rs. 60,000 respectively for 12 months. A take 10% of the profit for managing. The remaining profit of Rs. 1,40,000 is divided based on capital. What is B's share ?

P and Q started a business by investing Rs. 60,000 and Rs. 30,000 respectively. After 3 months, R joined with Rs. 45,000. At the end of the year, what is R's share in a profit of Rs. 40,000 (approx.)?
A, B, and C invest in the ratio 5 : 6 : 7. The time ratio is 4 : 3 : 2. What is the ratio of their profits ?

D and E start a business with equal investments. After 6 months, D adds Rs. 15,000 more. At the end of 1 year, if the total profit is Rs. 35,000, and E's share is Rs. 10,000, what was their initial investment?

P and Q start a business with Rs. 2,40,000 and Rs. 3,60,000 respectively. After 4 months, P adds Rs. 60,000 more, and Q withdraws Rs.60,000. At the end of 1 year, they make a profit of Rs. 2,40,000. What is Q's share of profit?
Partners X and Y invest Rs. 40,000 and Rs. 60,000 respectively. X works for 8 months, Y for 6 months. Profit is divided based on capital * time. Find their profit ratio.

'A' and 'B' entered a joint venture. 'A' brought Rs. 3,00,000 and 'B' Rs. 4,00,000. 'A' also contributed skilled labor equivalent to Rs. 1,00,000. At the end of the year, they earned Rs. 2,40,000 in profit. If they agreed to divide the profit in proportion to their effective contributions, what was A's share ?

P, Q, and R started a textile business. Their initial capital investments were ‚āĻ1,200,000, ‚āĻ800,000, and ‚āĻ1,000,000, respectively. After four months, P added ‚āĻ200,000, and R withdrew ‚āĻ100,000. Q kept his investment unchanged. The partnership lasted for 12 months (one year). What was the ratio of their effective capital contributions to profits ?
A man made a 20% profit on his investment. If he invested ‚āĻ10,000 and reinvested the profit, what would his net worth be after the second year at the same rate of return ?
If two partners invest for the same period but share profits in 3 : 5, and the total profit is Rs. 24,000, what is the capital of the second partner if the first invested Rs. 12,000?

Three friends, A, B, and C, invested in a start-up in the ratio 3 : 4 : 1. C managed the business and was entitled to receive an additional 20% of the total annual profit as a management fee. If the profit at the end of the year was ‚āĻ5,00,000, how much did B receive?

Three partners, A, B, and C started a firm with investments in the ratio 5:3:4. After 8 months, A doubled his capital, B tripled his, and C maintained his investment. If the total profit after one year was Rs. 2,35,000, what was B's share of profit ?
An investment is made in a business. In the first year, it gains 30%, in the second year, it loses 25%, and in the third year, it gains 20%. What is the overall gain or loss percentage ?
A starts a business with Rs. 4,000. B joins after 5 months with Rs. 8,000. If the profit at the end of the year is Rs. 5,800, what is B's share (approx.)?

Partners A and B entered into a business investing Rs. 50,000 and Rs. 80,000 respectively. After 5 months, C joins with Rs. 1,20,000. At year-end, profit was Rs. 100,000. How much more did C earn than A ?
Aman invested ‚āĻ60,000 for 10 months and Priya invested ‚āĻ90,000 for 8 months.If the total profit at the end of the year is ‚āĻ55,000, what is Priya‚Äôs share in the profit ?
A, B, C share a profit of ‚āĻ2400 in the ratio 3:4:5. If C's share is reduced by ‚āĻ200, and the remainder is equally divided between A and B, what will be B's final share?
A and B invest ‚āĻ1,00,000 and ‚āĻ1,50,000. After a year, the total profit, including 20% interest, is ‚āĻ48,000. What is B's share?
M and N invest ‚āĻ40,000 and ‚āĻ60,000. After a year, the total profit, including 10% interest, is ‚āĻ30,000. What is N's share?
A and B invest ‚āĻ15,000 and ‚āĻ10,000 respectively in a business. After 6 months, A increases his capital by 40%. If the total profit after 1 year is ‚āĻ1,26,000, what is B's share?
A and B invest ‚āĻ2,00,000 and ‚āĻ100,000, respectively. After one year, the total profit, which includes a 10% interest, amounts to ‚āĻ40,000. Determine B's share of the profit.
A and B make investments of ‚āĻ30,000 and ‚āĻ40,000, respectively. After one year, the total profit, which includes a 10% interest, amounts to ‚āĻ21,000. What portion of this profit does A receive?

A. B and C jointly started a business in which A invested ‚āĻ7,000 for 12 months, B invested ‚āĻ8,000 for 10 months and C invested ‚āĻ12,000 for 6 months. A was the working member for which he received 20% of the profits as his remuneration. The total profit earned at the end of one year was ‚āĻ14,750. What is C's share (in ‚āĻ) in the profit?
A, B and C invested money in a business such that 2 times A's share, 4 times B's share and 6 times C's share are all equal. If they earned ‚āĻ44,000, find the share of A.

Three partners decided to start a business. They decided to donate 10% of the profit to a charity organisation and then the remaining profit would be shared among first, second and third partner in the ratio 1 : 2 : 2. The total profit received was ‚āĻ1,80,000. Find the share of the first partner.

A and B enter into a partnership with ‚āĻ25,000 and ‚āĻ50,000, respectively, in a company for a year. After 7 months, C gets into a partnership with them with ‚āĻ30,000 and A withdraws his contribution after 9 months. What would be C's share in the profit, assuming a total profit of ‚āĻ3,900 at the end of the year?

Arvind, Bilal. Carmen invest ‚āĻ49,000 towards a business. Arvind invests ‚āĻ5,000 more than Bilal and Bilal invests ‚āĻ4,000 more than Carmen Out of a total profit of ‚āĻ98,000, how much does Bilal receive?
A , B and C started a business. A's capital is 7 times the capital of C and B's capital is 2 times the capital of A. If the profit is ‚āĻ7,920, then find the share of C (in ‚āĻ ).
Anam and Bikash invest in a business in the ratio 3 : 2. If in a particular month, Anam gets ‚āĻ3,840 as profit, the total profit is:
A, B and C started a business by investing ‚āĻ9,000, ‚āĻ18,000 and ‚āĻ 21,000, respectively. If B's share in the profit earned by them is ‚āĻ759, then the total profit earned by them together is:
A and B invested money in a business in the ratio of 7 : 5. If 15% of the total profit goes for charity, and A's share in the profit is Rs.5,950, then what is the total profit?
A starts a business with ‚āĻ45,000 and was joined afterwards by B with ‚āĻ30,000. When did B join if the profit at the end of the year was divided in the ratio 2 : 1 ?
A and B jointly start a business. The investment of A is equal to seven times the investment of B. Find the share of A (in ‚āĻ) in the annual profit of ‚āĻ2,75,000.
Rs.1200 are divided among X, Y and Z such that Y's share is Rs.200 more than X's share. Y's share is also Rs.200 less than Z's share. The share of Y is:

A and B started a business with Rs.15000 and Rs.30000 respectively. If A and B share profit in the ratio of 1: 5 respectively, then what is the ratio of the time period of investment for A and B respectively ?
